message.content:
I miss a document that explains (in terms for advanced user / high-level dev) the sequence of the steps executed when running the pc-switcher. Is not in README and not in docs/, not even in the gold docs/system.
Maybe best in README.md, as that is really overview documentation for user "What happens when I run pc-switcher".

Very concise, just for me to understand and remember: which steps, in which order, are executed.
E.g. needed to be able to reason about how actions of one step influence the environment the next step runs in.

- claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- code-simplifier:code-simplifier: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. Focuses on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise. (Tools: All tools)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- gsd-advisor-researcher: Researches a single gray area decision and returns a structured comparison table with rationale. Spawned by discuss-phase advisor mode. (Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*)

- gsd-ai-researcher: Researches a chosen AI framework's official docs to produce implementation-ready guidance — best practices, syntax, core patterns, and pitfalls distilled for the specific use case. Writes the Framework Quick Reference and Implementation Guidance sections of AI-SPEC.md. Spawned by /gsd-ai-integration-phase orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebFetch, WebSearch, mcp__context7__*)
- gsd-assumptions-analyzer: Deeply analyzes codebase for a phase and returns structured assumptions with evidence. Spawned by discuss-phase assumptions mode. (Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill)

- gsd-code-fixer: Applies fixes to code review findings from REVIEW.md. Reads source files, applies intelligent fixes, and commits each fix atomically. Spawned by /gsd-code-review --fix. (Tools: Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill)

- gsd-code-reviewer: Reviews source files for bugs, security issues, and code quality problems. Produces structured REVIEW.md with severity-classified findings. Spawned by /gsd-code-review. (Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill)

- gsd-codebase-mapper: Explores codebase and writes structured analysis documents. Spawned by map-codebase with a focus area (tech, arch, quality, concerns). Writes documents directly to reduce orchestrator context load. (Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Write, Skill)

- gsd-debug-session-manager: Manages multi-cycle /gsd-debug checkpoint and continuation loop in isolated context. Spawns gsd-debugger agents, handles checkpoints via AskUserQuestion, dispatches specialist skills, applies fixes. Returns compact summary to main context. Spawned by /gsd-debug command. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Agent, AskUserQuestion)

- gsd-debugger: Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd-debug orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill, WebSearch)

- gsd-doc-classifier: Classifies a single planning document as ADR, PRD, SPEC, DOC, or UNKNOWN. Extracts title, scope summary, and cross-references. Spawned in parallel by /gsd-ingest-docs. Writes a JSON classification file and returns a one-line confirmation. (Tools: Read, Write, Grep, Glob)

- gsd-doc-synthesizer: Synthesizes classified planning docs into a single consolidated context. Applies precedence rules, detects cross-ref cycles, enforces LOCKED-vs-LOCKED hard-blocks, and writes INGEST-CONFLICTS.md with three buckets (auto-resolved, competing-variants, unresolved-blockers). Spawned by /gsd-ingest-docs. (Tools: Read, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash)
- gsd-doc-verifier: Verifies factual claims in generated docs against the live codebase. Returns structured JSON per doc. (Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob)

- gsd-doc-writer: Writes and updates project documentation. Spawned with a doc_assignment block specifying doc type, mode (create/update/supplement), and project context. (Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Write, Edit, Skill)

- gsd-domain-researcher: Researches the business domain and real-world application context of the AI system being built. Surfaces domain expert evaluation criteria, industry-specific failure modes, regulatory context, and what "good" looks like for practitioners in this field — before the eval-planner turns it into measurable rubrics. Spawned by /gsd-ai-integration-phase orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*)

- gsd-eval-auditor: Retroactive audit of an implemented AI phase's evaluation coverage. Checks implementation against the AI-SPEC.md evaluation plan. Scores each eval dimension as COVERED/PARTIAL/MISSING. Produces a scored EVAL-REVIEW.md with findings, gaps, and remediation guidance. Spawned by /gsd-eval-review orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill)

- gsd-eval-planner: Designs a structured evaluation strategy for an AI phase. Identifies critical failure modes, selects eval dimensions with rubrics, recommends tooling, and specifies the reference dataset. Writes the Evaluation Strategy, Guardrails, and Production Monitoring sections of AI-SPEC.md. Spawned by /gsd-ai-integration-phase orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, AskUserQuestion)

- gsd-executor: Executes GSD plans with atomic commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and state management. Spawned by execute-phase orchestrator or execute-plan command. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill, mcp__context7__*)

- gsd-framework-selector: Presents an interactive decision matrix to surface the right AI/LLM framework for the user's specific use case. Produces a scored recommendation with rationale. Spawned by /gsd-ai-integration-phase and /gsd-select-framework orchestrators. (Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, AskUserQuestion)
- gsd-integration-checker: Verifies cross-phase integration and E2E flows. Checks that phases connect properly and user workflows complete end-to-end. (Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill)
- gsd-intel-updater: Analyzes codebase and writes structured intel files to .planning/intel/. (Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Glob, Grep)

- gsd-mempalace-curator: Ship-time MemPalace curation — writes the session diary, proposes/creates cross-project tunnels, mirrors extract-learnings into the temporal KG, and runs wing-scoped drawer pruning. Spawned at ship:post by the mempalace capability. (Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob)
- gsd-nyquist-auditor: Fills Nyquist validation gaps by generating tests and verifying coverage for phase requirements (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep, Skill)

- gsd-pattern-mapper: Analyzes codebase for existing patterns and produces PATTERNS.md mapping new files to closest analogs. Read-only codebase analysis spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator before planning. (Tools: Read, Bash, Glob, Grep, Write)

- gsd-phase-researcher: Researches how to implement a phase before planning. Produces RESEARCH.md consumed by gsd-planner. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*, mcp__firecrawl__*, mcp__exa__*, mcp__tavily__*, mcp__ref__*, mcp__jina__*, mcp__perplexity__*)
- gsd-plan-checker: Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Bash, Glob, Grep, Skill)

- gsd-planner: Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep, Skill, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*)

- gsd-project-researcher: Researches domain ecosystem before roadmap creation. Produces files in .planning/research/ consumed during roadmap creation. Spawned by /gsd-new-project or /gsd-new-milestone orchestrators. (Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*, mcp__firecrawl__*, mcp__exa__*, mcp__tavily__*, mcp__ref__*, mcp__jina__*, mcp__perplexity__*)

- gsd-research-synthesizer: Synthesizes research outputs from parallel researcher agents into SUMMARY.md. Spawned by /gsd-new-project after 4 researcher agents complete. (Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Skill)

- gsd-roadmapper: Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd-new-project orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Glob, Grep, Skill)

- gsd-security-auditor: Verifies threat mitigations from PLAN.md threat model exist in implemented code. Produces SECURITY.md. Spawned by /gsd-secure-phase. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep, Skill)
- gsd-ui-auditor: Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code. Produces scored UI-REVIEW.md. Spawned by /gsd-ui-review orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill)
- gsd-ui-checker: Validates UI-SPEC.md design contracts against 6 quality dimensions. Produces BLOCK/FLAG/PASS verdicts. Spawned by /gsd-ui-phase orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Bash, Glob, Grep, Skill)

- gsd-ui-researcher: Produces UI-SPEC.md design contract for frontend phases. Reads upstream artifacts, detects design system state, asks only unanswered questions. Spawned by /gsd-ui-phase orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*, mcp__firecrawl__*, mcp__exa__*, mcp__tavily__*, mcp__ref__*, mcp__jina__*)

- gsd-user-profiler: Analyzes extracted session messages across 8 behavioral dimensions to produce a scored developer profile with confidence levels and evidence. Spawned by profile orchestration workflows. (Tools: Read)

- gsd-verifier: Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report. (Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer: Use this agent when you need to review code for adherence to project guidelines, style guides, and best practices. This agent should be used proactively after writing or modifying code, especially before committing changes or creating pull requests. It will check for style violations, potential issues, and ensure code follows the established patterns in CLAUDE.md. Also the agent needs to know which files to focus on for the review. In most cases this will be recently completed work which is unstaged in git (can be retrieved by running git diff). However there can be cases where this is different, make sure to specify this as the agent input when calling the agent. Typical triggers include the user asking for a review of a feature they just implemented, the assistant proactively reviewing its own newly-written code before declaring a task done, and a final pre-PR check before opening a pull request. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-simplifier: Use this agent when code has been written or modified and needs to be simplified for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. This agent should be triggered automatically after completing a coding task or writing a logical chunk of code. It simplifies code by following project best practices while retaining all functionality. The agent focuses only on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise.

Examples:

<example>
Context: The assistant has just implemented a new feature that adds user authentication to an API endpoint.
user: "Please add authentication to the /api/users endpoint"
assistant: "I've implemented the authentication for the /api/users endpoint. Here's the code:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
Since a logical chunk of code has been written (authentication feature), use the Task tool to launch the code-simplifier agent to improve the code's clarity and maintainability while preserving functionality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now let me use the code-simplifier agent to refine this implementation for better clarity and maintainability"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just fixed a bug by adding several conditional checks.
user: "Fix the null pointer exception in the data processor"
assistant: "I've added the necessary null checks to prevent the exception:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After modifying code to fix a bug, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the fix follows best practices and maintains code quality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Let me refine this bug fix using the code-simplifier agent to ensure it follows our best practices"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just refactored a function to improve performance.
user: "Optimize the data sorting algorithm for better performance"
assistant: "I've optimized the sorting algorithm. Here's the updated implementation:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After completing a performance optimization task, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and maintainable.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now I'll use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and follows our coding standards"
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:comment-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to analyze code comments for accuracy, completeness, and long-term maintainability. This includes (1) after generating large documentation comments or docstrings, (2) before finalizing a pull request that adds or modifies comments, (3) when reviewing existing comments for potential technical debt or comment rot, and (4) when you need to verify that comments accurately reflect the code they describe. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:pr-test-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to review a pull request for test coverage quality and completeness. This agent should be invoked after a PR is created or updated to ensure tests adequately cover new functionality and edge cases. Typical triggers include the user asking whether tests on a freshly-created PR are thorough, an updated PR adding new logic that needs coverage analysis, and a final pre-merge double-check before marking a PR ready. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:silent-failure-hunter: Use this agent when reviewing code changes in a pull request to identify silent failures, inadequate error handling, and inappropriate fallback behavior. This agent should be invoked proactively after completing a logical chunk of work that involves error handling, catch blocks, fallback logic, or any code that could potentially suppress errors. Examples:

<example>
Context: Daisy has just finished implementing a new feature that fetches data from an API with fallback behavior.
Daisy: "I've added error handling to the API client. Can you review it?"
Assistant: "Let me use the silent-failure-hunter agent to thoroughly examine the error handling in your changes."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has created a PR with changes that include try-catch blocks.
Daisy: "Please review PR #1234"
Assistant: "I'll use the silent-failure-hunter agent to check for any silent failures or inadequate error handling in this PR."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has just refactored error handling code.
Daisy: "I've updated the error handling in the authentication module"
Assistant: "Let me proactively use the silent-failure-hunter agent to ensure the error handling changes don't introduce silent failures."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:type-design-analyzer: Use this agent when you need expert analysis of type design in your codebase. Specifically use it (1) when introducing a new type to ensure it follows best practices for encapsulation and invariant expression, (2) during pull request creation to review all types being added, and (3) when refactoring existing types to improve their design quality. The agent will provide both qualitative feedback and quantitative ratings on encapsulation, invariant expression, usefulness, and enforcement. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- pr-review-toolkit:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".

- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). 1	# PC-switcher
2	
3	A synchronization system for seamless switching between Linux desktop machines. Keep your laptops and workstations in sync with near-complete system-state replication.
4	
5	## Overview
6	
7	PC-switcher enables a simple workflow: work on one machine, sync before switching, resume on the other—without manual file management or cloud sync overhead.
8	
9	```plain
10	Work on source machine → Trigger sync → Resume on target machine
11	```
12	
13	**Status**: Core infrastructure complete. Core sync functionality in development.
14	
15	## What Gets Synced
16	
17	- **User data**: `/home`, `/root` with all documents, code, configs, and selective caches
18	- **Packages**: apt, snap, flatpak, PPAs, manual installs
19	- **Application configurations**: GNOME, cloud mounts, systemd services
20	- **System configurations**: Machine-independent `/etc` files, users, groups
21	- **File metadata**: Owner, permissions, ACLs, timestamps
22	- **Containers & VMs**: Docker images/containers/volumes, KVM/virt-manager VMs
23	- **k3s**: Local single-node cluster state and PVCs
24	
25	**Never synced**: SSH keys, Tailscale config, GPU/hardware caches, machine-specific packages
26	
27	## Installation
28	
29	Install using the installation script:
30	```bash
31	curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/flaksit/pc-switcher/refs/heads/main/install.sh | bash
32	```
33	
34	To install a specific version:
35	```bash
36	curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/flaksit/pc-switcher/refs/heads/main/install.sh | VERSION=0.2.0 bash
37	```
38	
39	Test installation:
40	```bash
41	pc-switcher --help
42	pc-switcher --version
43	```
44	
45	**Uninstalling**:
46	```bash
47	uv tool uninstall pcswitcher
48	```
49	(Note: Use the Python package name `pcswitcher`, not the command name `pc-switcher`)
50	
51	After installation, create the default configuration:
52	```bash
53	pc-switcher init
54	```
55	
56	## Quick Start
57	
58	Before syncing, ensure:
59	- Target machine is powered on and reachable via SSH (LAN, VPN, etc.)
60	- You're logged out from the desktop (or close all apps)
61	
62	Trigger a sync:
63	```bash
64	pc-switcher sync <target-hostname>
65	```
66	
67	Monitor sync progress with:
68	```bash
69	pc-switcher logs
70	```
71	
72	After sync completes, power off the source machine and resume work on target.
73	
74	## Configuration
75	
76	Run `pc-switcher init` to create the default configuration file at `~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml`, or create it manually:
77	
78	```yaml
79	# Logging configuration (see Logging Configuration section below for details)
80	logging:
81	  file: DEBUG      # Floor level for file output
82	  tui: INFO        # Floor level for terminal output
83	  external: WARNING  # Floor for third-party libraries
84	
85	# Sync jobs (true = enabled, false = disabled)
86	sync_jobs:
87	  dummy_success: true
88	  dummy_fail: false
89	
90	# Disk space monitoring
91	disk_space_monitor:
92	  preflight_minimum: "20%"  # Or absolute like "50GiB"
93	  runtime_minimum: "15%"    # CRITICAL abort if below
94	  warning_threshold: "25%"  # WARNING log if below
95	  check_interval: 30        # Seconds
96	
97	# Btrfs snapshots
98	btrfs_snapshots:
99	  subvolumes:
100	    - "@"
101	    - "@home"
102	  keep_recent: 3
103	  # max_age_days: 30  # Optional - enables age-based cleanup
104	```
105	
106	See default configuration in `src/pcswitcher/default-config.yaml`.
107	
108	## Logging Configuration
109	
110	Configure log levels in your `~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml`:
111	
112	```yaml
113	logging:
114	  file: DEBUG      # Log level floor for file output (default: DEBUG)
115	  tui: INFO        # Log level floor for TUI output (default: INFO)
116	  external: WARNING  # Log level floor for external libraries (default: WARNING)
117	```
118	
119	### Log Levels
120	
121	| Level | Value | Description |
122	|-------|-------|-------------|
123	| DEBUG | 10 | Internal diagnostics |
124	| FULL | 15 | Operational details (file-level sync info) |
125	| INFO | 20 | High-level operations (job start/complete) |
126	| WARNING | 30 | Unexpected but non-fatal conditions |
127	| ERROR | 40 | Recoverable errors |
128	| CRITICAL | 50 | Unrecoverable errors, sync must abort |
129	
130	### Default Behavior
131	
132	- `file: DEBUG` - All log levels written to file
133	- `tui: INFO` - Only INFO and above shown in terminal
134	- `external: WARNING` - External library logs (asyncssh, etc.) filtered to WARNING+
135	
136	Log files are written to `~/.local/share/pc-switcher/logs/` in JSON lines format.
137	
138	### Common Configurations
139	
140	**Debug SSH connection issues:**
141	```yaml
142	logging:
143	  file: DEBUG
144	  tui: INFO
145	  external: DEBUG  # Show asyncssh debug logs
146	```
147	
148	**Quiet mode (errors only):**
149	```yaml
150	logging:
151	  file: DEBUG     # Still log everything to file
152	  tui: ERROR      # Only show errors in terminal
153	  external: ERROR
154	```
155	
156	## Available Commands
157	
158	```bash
159	# Initialize configuration file
160	pc-switcher init [--force]    # Create default config at ~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml
161	
162	# Sync to target machine
163	pc-switcher sync <target-hostname> [--config PATH]
164	
165	# View logs
166	pc-switcher logs              # Show logs directory and list recent logs
167	pc-switcher logs --last       # Show path to most recent log file
168	
169	# Clean up old snapshots
170	pc-switcher cleanup-snapshots --older-than 7d [--dry-run]
171	
172	# Self-update pc-switcher
173	pc-switcher self update [VERSION] [--prerelease]
174	```
175	
176	## Requirements
177	
178	- Ubuntu 24.04 LTS on all machines
179	- Single btrfs filesystem (all synced data on one filesystem per machine)
180	- SSH access between machines (LAN, VPN such as Tailscale, or other network)
181	- Only one machine actively used at a time
182	
183	## Key Design Principles
184	
185	1. **Reliability**: No data loss, conflict detection, consistent state, full auditability
186	2. **Smooth UX**: Single command to launch entire sync; minimal manual intervention
187	3. **Use standard tools**: Well-supported, maintainable approach
188	4. **Minimize disk wear**: NVMe SSDs—avoid unnecessary writes
189	5. **Simplicity**: Easy to understand, modify, and maintain
190	
191	## Troubleshooting
192	
193	### GitHub API Rate Limits
194	
195	When running `pc-switcher --version`, `self update`, or sync (which installs pc-switcher on target), you may see rate limit errors like:
196	
197	```
198	RuntimeError: Failed to fetch GitHub releases: 403 {"message": "API rate limit exceeded..."}
199	```
200	
201	This happens because pc-switcher queries the GitHub API to check for releases. Unauthenticated requests are limited to 60/hour.
202	
203	**Solution**: Add a GitHub personal access token with public read-only permissions to your `~/.profile` on both source and target machines:
204	
205	```bash
206	echo 'export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here' >> ~/.profile
207	source ~/.profile
208	```
209	
210	With a token, the rate limit increases to 5,000 requests/hour.
211	
212	## Documentation

215

219	- **[Architecture Decision Records](docs/adr/_index.md)** - Design decisions and rationale
220	- **[Development Guide](docs/dev/development-guide.md)** - Development workflow and guidelines

# PC-switcher

A synchronization system for seamless switching between Linux desktop machines. Keep your laptops and workstations in sync with near-complete system-state replication.

## Overview

PC-switcher enables a simple workflow: work on one machine, sync before switching, resume on the other—without manual file management or cloud sync overhead.

```plain
Work on source machine → Trigger sync → Resume on target machine
```

**Status**: Core infrastructure complete. Core sync functionality in development.

## What Gets Synced

- **User data**: `/home`, `/root` with all documents, code, configs, and selective caches
- **Packages**: apt, snap, flatpak, PPAs, manual installs
- **Application configurations**: GNOME, cloud mounts, systemd services
- **System configurations**: Machine-independent `/etc` files, users, groups
- **File metadata**: Owner, permissions, ACLs, timestamps
- **Containers & VMs**: Docker images/containers/volumes, KVM/virt-manager VMs
- **k3s**: Local single-node cluster state and PVCs

**Never synced**: SSH keys, Tailscale config, GPU/hardware caches, machine-specific packages

## Installation

Install using the installation script:
```bash
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/flaksit/pc-switcher/refs/heads/main/install.sh | bash
```

To install a specific version:
```bash
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/flaksit/pc-switcher/refs/heads/main/install.sh | VERSION=0.2.0 bash
```

Test installation:
```bash
pc-switcher --help
pc-switcher --version
```

**Uninstalling**:
```bash
uv tool uninstall pcswitcher
```
(Note: Use the Python package name `pcswitcher`, not the command name `pc-switcher`)

After installation, create the default configuration:
```bash
pc-switcher init
```

## Quick Start

Before syncing, ensure:
- Target machine is powered on and reachable via SSH (LAN, VPN, etc.)
- You're logged out from the desktop (or close all apps)

Trigger a sync:
```bash
pc-switcher sync <target-hostname>
```

Monitor sync progress with:
```bash
pc-switcher logs
```

After sync completes, power off the source machine and resume work on target.

## Configuration

Run `pc-switcher init` to create the default configuration file at `~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml`, or create it manually:

```yaml
# Logging configuration (see Logging Configuration section below for details)
logging:
  file: DEBUG      # Floor level for file output
  tui: INFO        # Floor level for terminal output
  external: WARNING  # Floor for third-party libraries

# Sync jobs (true = enabled, false = disabled)
sync_jobs:
  dummy_success: true
  dummy_fail: false

# Disk space monitoring
disk_space_monitor:
  preflight_minimum: "20%"  # Or absolute like "50GiB"
  runtime_minimum: "15%"    # CRITICAL abort if below
  warning_threshold: "25%"  # WARNING log if below
  check_interval: 30        # Seconds

# Btrfs snapshots
btrfs_snapshots:
  subvolumes:
    - "@"
    - "@home"
  keep_recent: 3
  # max_age_days: 30  # Optional - enables age-based cleanup
```

See default configuration in `src/pcswitcher/default-config.yaml`.

## Logging Configuration

Configure log levels in your `~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml`:

```yaml
logging:
  file: DEBUG      # Log level floor for file output (default: DEBUG)
  tui: INFO        # Log level floor for TUI output (default: INFO)
  external: WARNING  # Log level floor for external libraries (default: WARNING)
```

### Log Levels

| Level | Value | Description |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| DEBUG | 10 | Internal diagnostics |
| FULL | 15 | Operational details (file-level sync info) |
| INFO | 20 | High-level operations (job start/complete) |
| WARNING | 30 | Unexpected but non-fatal conditions |
| ERROR | 40 | Recoverable errors |
| CRITICAL | 50 | Unrecoverable errors, sync must abort |

### Default Behavior

- `file: DEBUG` - All log levels written to file
- `tui: INFO` - Only INFO and above shown in terminal
- `external: WARNING` - External library logs (asyncssh, etc.) filtered to WARNING+

Log files are written to `~/.local/share/pc-switcher/logs/` in JSON lines format.

### Common Configurations

**Debug SSH connection issues:**
```yaml
logging:
  file: DEBUG
  tui: INFO
  external: DEBUG  # Show asyncssh debug logs
```

**Quiet mode (errors only):**
```yaml
logging:
  file: DEBUG     # Still log everything to file
  tui: ERROR      # Only show errors in terminal
  external: ERROR
```

## Available Commands

```bash
# Initialize configuration file
pc-switcher init [--force]    # Create default config at ~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml

# Sync to target machine
pc-switcher sync <target-hostname> [--config PATH]

# View logs
pc-switcher logs              # Show logs directory and list recent logs
pc-switcher logs --last       # Show path to most recent log file

# Clean up old snapshots
pc-switcher cleanup-snapshots --older-than 7d [--dry-run]

# Self-update pc-switcher
pc-switcher self update [VERSION] [--prerelease]
```

## Requirements

- Ubuntu 24.04 LTS on all machines
- Single btrfs filesystem (all synced data on one filesystem per machine)
- SSH access between machines (LAN, VPN such as Tailscale, or other network)
- Only one machine actively used at a time

## Key Design Principles

1. **Reliability**: No data loss, conflict detection, consistent state, full auditability
2. **Smooth UX**: Single command to launch entire sync; minimal manual intervention
3. **Use standard tools**: Well-supported, maintainable approach
4. **Minimize disk wear**: NVMe SSDs—avoid unnecessary writes
5. **Simplicity**: Easy to understand, modify, and maintain

## Troubleshooting

### GitHub API Rate Limits

When running `pc-switcher --version`, `self update`, or sync (which installs pc-switcher on target), you may see rate limit errors like:

```
RuntimeError: Failed to fetch GitHub releases: 403 {"message": "API rate limit exceeded..."}
```

This happens because pc-switcher queries the GitHub API to check for releases. Unauthenticated requests are limited to 60/hour.

**Solution**: Add a GitHub personal access token with public read-only permissions to your `~/.profile` on both source and target machines:

```bash
echo 'export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here' >> ~/.profile
source ~/.profile
```

With a token, the rate limit increases to 5,000 requests/hour.

## Documentation

See [docs/README.md](docs/README.md) for the documentation index.

Key documents:
- **[High level requirements](docs/planning/High%20level%20requirements.md)** - Project vision, scope, workflow
- **[Architecture](docs/system/architecture.md)** - System architecture and design
- **[Architecture Decision Records](docs/adr/_index.md)** - Design decisions and rationale
- **[Development Guide](docs/dev/development-guide.md)** - Development workflow and guidelines

## Development

Clone the repository:
```bash
git clone git@github.com:flaksit/pc-switcher.git
cd pc-switcher
```

Install dependencies:
```bash
uv sync
```

PR workflow:
- If a PR fixes an issue, include `Fixes #<issue>` (or `Closes` / `Resolves`) in the PR description/title so GitHub closes the issue on merge.
- See [docs/dev/development-guide.md](docs/dev/development-guide.md).

Install the tool from your local checkout (for testing):
```bash
./install.sh
```
This auto-detects the git workspace and runs `uv tool install .` from your local code.

If you want to install a specific version from GitHub, use one of the following commands:
```bash
# use the local script to install a specific package version from GitHub
VERSION=0.2.0 ./install.sh
./install.sh --ref abcd0123
./install.sh --ref my_feature_branch
# Use the install script from GitHub to install a specific package version
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/flaksit/pc-switcher/refs/heads/main/install.sh | bash -s -- --ref my_feature_branch
```


Run code quality checks:
```bash
uv run ruff format .    # Format code
uv run ruff check .     # Lint
uv run basedpyright     # Type check
uv run pytest           # Run tests
```

This project uses **SpecKit**—a specification-driven workflow via custom slash commands:

```bash
/speckit.specify "feature description"  # Create feature spec
/speckit.clarify                        # Refine spec details
/speckit.plan                           # Generate design artifacts
/speckit.tasks                          # Create actionable tasks
/speckit.analyze                        # Check consistency specs-plan-tasks
/speckit.implement                      # Execute implementation
```

See [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) for complete development workflow details.

1	"""Command execution for local and remote machines."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import asyncio
6	import shlex
7	from collections.abc import AsyncGenerator, AsyncIterator
8	from pathlib import Path
9	from typing import Protocol
10	
11	import asyncssh
12	
13	from pcswitcher.models import CommandResult
14	
15	__all__ = [
16	    "BashLoginRemoteExecutor",
17	    "Executor",
18	    "LocalExecutor",
19	    "LocalProcess",
20	    "Process",
21	    "RemoteExecutor",
22	    "RemoteProcess",
23	]
24	
25	
26	class Executor(Protocol):
27	    """Protocol for command execution on local or remote machines.
28	
29	    Both LocalExecutor and RemoteExecutor implement this protocol,
30	    allowing code to work with either without knowing which one it is.
31	    """
32	
33	    async def run_command(
34	        self,
35	        cmd: str,
36	        timeout: float | None = None,
37	    ) -> CommandResult:
38	        """Run a command and wait for completion."""
39	        ...
40	
41	    async def terminate_all_processes(self) -> None:
42	        """Terminate all tracked processes."""
43	        ...
44	
45	
46	class Process(Protocol):
47	    """Handle for a running process with streaming output.
48	
49	    Note: stdin is intentionally not supported. All commands must be
50	    non-interactive. This is a design constraint to ensure reliable
51	    automated execution without prompts or user input requirements.
52	    """
53

"""Command execution for local and remote machines."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import shlex
from collections.abc import AsyncGenerator, AsyncIterator
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Protocol

import asyncssh

from pcswitcher.models import CommandResult

__all__ = [
    "BashLoginRemoteExecutor",
    "Executor",
    "LocalExecutor",
    "LocalProcess",
    "Process",
    "RemoteExecutor",
    "RemoteProcess",
]


class Executor(Protocol):
    """Protocol for command execution on local or remote machines.

    Both LocalExecutor and RemoteExecutor implement this protocol,
    allowing code to work with either without knowing which one it is.
    """

    async def run_command(
        self,
        cmd: str,
        timeout: float | None = None,
    ) -> CommandResult:
        """Run a command and wait for completion."""
        ...

    async def terminate_all_processes(self) -> None:
        """Terminate all tracked processes."""
        ...


class Process(Protocol):
    """Handle for a running process with streaming output.

    Note: stdin is intentionally not supported. All commands must be
    non-interactive. This is a design constraint to ensure reliable
    automated execution without prompts or user input requirements.
    """

    async def stdout(self) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        """Iterate over stdout lines as they arrive."""
        ...

    async def stderr(self) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        """Iterate over stderr lines as they arrive."""
        ...

    async def wait(self) -> CommandResult:
        """Wait for process to complete and return result."""
        ...

    async def terminate(self) -> None:
        """Terminate the process."""
        ...


class LocalProcess:
    """Process wrapper for local asyncio subprocess."""

    def __init__(self, proc: asyncio.subprocess.Process) -> None:
        self._proc = proc

    async def stdout(self) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        """Iterate over stdout lines as they arrive."""
        if self._proc.stdout is None:
            return
        async for line in self._proc.stdout:
            yield line.decode()

    async def stderr(self) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        """Iterate over stderr lines as they arrive."""
        if self._proc.stderr is None:
            return
        async for line in self._proc.stderr:
            yield line.decode()

    async def wait(self) -> CommandResult:
        """Wait for process to complete and return result."""
        stdout_bytes, stderr_bytes = await self._proc.communicate()
        return CommandResult(
            exit_code=self._proc.returncode or 0,
            stdout=stdout_bytes.decode() if stdout_bytes else "",
            stderr=stderr_bytes.decode() if stderr_bytes else "",
        )

    async def read_stdout_chunks(self, size: int = 4096) -> AsyncGenerator[bytes]:
        """Yield raw stdout bytes in chunks until EOF.

        Use instead of the line-based `stdout()` iterator when the process
        writes carriage-return-delimited output (e.g. rsync `--info=progress2`)
        that would block a readline-based reader indefinitely (RESEARCH Pitfall 2).

        Args:
            size: Number of bytes to read per chunk.

        Yields:
            Raw bytes chunks as they arrive; stops at EOF.
        """
        if self._proc.stdout is None:
            return
        while True:
            chunk = await self._proc.stdout.read(size)
            if not chunk:
                break
            yield chunk

    async def wait_result(self) -> CommandResult:
        """Wait for process exit and return the result, assuming stdout already consumed.

        Reads stderr to completion (via the stderr pipe) and then waits for the
        process to exit.  The `stdout` field in the returned `CommandResult` is
        empty because stdout was already consumed by `read_stdout_chunks`.

        Use this after draining stdout via `read_stdout_chunks` to obtain the
        exit code and any error output without calling `communicate()` (which
        would attempt to read stdout a second time).
        """
        stderr_bytes = b""
        if self._proc.stderr is not None:
            stderr_bytes = await self._proc.stderr.read()
        await self._proc.wait()
        return CommandResult(
            exit_code=self._proc.returncode or 0,
            stdout="",
            stderr=stderr_bytes.decode(errors="replace"),
        )

    async def terminate(self) -> None:
        """Terminate the process."""
        self._proc.terminate()
        await self._proc.wait()


class LocalExecutor:
    """Executes commands on the source machine via async subprocess."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._processes: list[asyncio.subprocess.Process] = []

    async def run_command(
        self,
        cmd: str,
        timeout: float | None = None,
    ) -> CommandResult:
        """Run a command and wait for completion.

        Args:
            cmd: Shell command to execute
            timeout: Optional timeout in seconds

        Returns:
            CommandResult with exit code, stdout, and stderr
        """
        proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_shell(
            cmd,
            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
        )
        try:
            stdout, stderr = await asyncio.wait_for(
                proc.communicate(),
                timeout=timeout,
            )
            return CommandResult(
                exit_code=proc.returncode or 0,
                stdout=stdout.decode() if stdout else "",
                stderr=stderr.decode() if stderr else "",
            )
        except TimeoutError:
            proc.terminate()
            await proc.wait()
            raise

    async def start_process(self, cmd: str) -> LocalProcess:
        """Start a long-running process with streaming output.

        Args:
            cmd: Shell command to execute

        Returns:
            LocalProcess wrapper for the subprocess
        """
        proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_shell(
            cmd,
            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
        )
        self._processes.append(proc)
        return LocalProcess(proc)

    async def terminate_all_processes(self) -> None:
        """Terminate all tracked processes."""
        for proc in self._processes:
            if proc.returncode is None:  # Still running
                proc.terminate()
        # Wait for all to finish
        await asyncio.gather(
            *(proc.wait() for proc in self._processes if proc.returncode is None),
            return_exceptions=True,
        )
        self._processes.clear()


class RemoteProcess:
    """Process wrapper for SSH remote process."""

    def __init__(self, proc: asyncssh.SSHClientProcess[str]) -> None:
        self._proc = proc

    async def stdout(self) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        """Iterate over stdout lines as they arrive."""
        async for line in self._proc.stdout:
            yield line

    async def stderr(self) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        """Iterate over stderr lines as they arrive."""
        async for line in self._proc.stderr:
            yield line

    async def wait(self) -> CommandResult:
        """Wait for process to complete and return result."""
        await self._proc.wait()
        # Read remaining output after process completes
        stdout_data = await self._proc.stdout.read()
        stderr_data = await self._proc.stderr.read()
        return CommandResult(
            exit_code=self._proc.exit_status or 0,
            stdout=stdout_data,
            stderr=stderr_data,
        )

    async def terminate(self) -> None:
        """Terminate the process."""
        self._proc.terminate()
        await self._proc.wait()


class RemoteExecutor:
    """Executes commands on target machine via SSH connection."""

    def __init__(self, conn: asyncssh.SSHClientConnection) -> None:
        self._conn = conn
        self._processes: list[asyncssh.SSHClientProcess[str]] = []
        self._default_login_shell = False

    def _wrap_for_login_shell(self, cmd: str) -> str:
        """Wrap command for execution in bash login shell.

        Remote SSH commands run in non-login shells by default, which means
        ~/.profile isn't sourced and PATH may not include ~/.local/bin.

        This wrapper ensures commands run with full user environment by
        wrapping them in 'bash -l -c "..."', which:
        - Sources /etc/profile and ~/.profile (login shell behavior)
        - Ensures PATH includes user-installed tools (uv, pc-switcher)
        - Simulates interactive SSH session environment

        Args:
            cmd: Original shell command

        Returns:
            Wrapped command with bash -l -c prefix and proper quoting
        """
        return f"bash -l -c {shlex.quote(cmd)}"

    async def run_command(
        self,
        cmd: str,
        timeout: float | None = None,
        login_shell: bool | None = None,
    ) -> CommandResult:
        """Run a command on remote machine and wait for completion.

        Args:
            cmd: Shell command to execute
            timeout: Optional timeout in seconds
            login_shell: If True, wrap command in 'bash -l -c' to source ~/.profile
                and ensure proper PATH. If None, uses the executor's default.
                Useful for commands requiring user-installed tools (e.g., uv, pc-switcher).

        Returns:
            CommandResult with exit code, stdout, and stderr

        Note:
            Using login_shell=True adds overhead (typically 10-50ms per command)
            due to profile sourcing. Only use when environment setup is needed.
        """
        use_login_shell = login_shell if login_shell is not None else self._default_login_shell
        if use_login_shell:
            cmd = self._wrap_for_login_shell(cmd)

        try:
            result = await asyncio.wait_for(
                self._conn.run(cmd),
                timeout=timeout,
            )
            return CommandResult(
                exit_code=result.exit_status or 0,
                stdout=str(result.stdout) if result.stdout else "",
                stderr=str(result.stderr) if result.stderr else "",
            )
        except TimeoutError:
            raise

    async def start_process(self, cmd: str, login_shell: bool | None = None) -> RemoteProcess:
        """Start a long-running process on remote machine.

        Args:
            cmd: Shell command to execute
            login_shell: If True, wrap command in 'bash -l -c' to source ~/.profile
                and ensure proper PATH. If None, uses the executor's default.
                Useful for background processes requiring user-installed tools.

        Returns:
            RemoteProcess wrapper for the SSH process

        Note:
            Using login_shell=True adds startup overhead. Only use when
            environment setup is needed for the background process.
        """
        use_login_shell = login_shell if login_shell is not None else self._default_login_shell
        if use_login_shell:
            cmd = self._wrap_for_login_shell(cmd)

        process = await self._conn.create_process(cmd)
        self._processes.append(process)
        return RemoteProcess(process)

    async def terminate_all_processes(self) -> None:
        """Terminate all tracked remote processes."""
        for process in self._processes:
            process.terminate()
        # Wait for all to finish
        await asyncio.gather(
            *(proc.wait() for proc in self._processes),
            return_exceptions=True,
        )
        self._processes.clear()

    async def send_file(self, local: Path, remote: str) -> None:
        """Copy a file from local machine to remote.

        Args:
            local: Local file path
            remote: Remote destination path
        """
        async with self._conn.start_sftp_client() as sftp:
            await sftp.put(str(local), remote)

    async def get_file(self, remote: str, local: Path) -> None:
        """Copy a file from remote machine to local.

        Args:
            remote: Remote file path
            local: Local destination path
        """
        async with self._conn.start_sftp_client() as sftp:
            await sftp.get(remote, str(local))


class BashLoginRemoteExecutor(RemoteExecutor):
    """RemoteExecutor that runs all commands in bash login shell by default.

    This executor wraps all commands in 'bash -l -c "..."' to ensure:
    - ~/.profile is sourced
    - PATH includes ~/.local/bin (for user-installed tools like uv, pc-switcher)
    - Environment matches interactive SSH sessions

    Primarily used in integration tests where all commands interact with
    user-installed tools. Production code should use base RemoteExecutor
    and explicitly pass login_shell=True only when needed.

    The login_shell parameter can still be overridden per-call:
        executor.run_command("sudo systemctl status", login_shell=False)
    """

    def __init__(self, conn: asyncssh.SSHClientConnection) -> None:
        """Initialize with login shell enabled by default."""
        super().__init__(conn)
        self._default_login_shell = True

63	class Orchestrator:
64	    """Main orchestrator coordinating the complete sync workflow.
65	
66	    Responsibilities:
67	    - Schema and job config validation
68	    - SSH connection management
69	    - Lock acquisition (source and target)
70	    - Version check and self-installation
71	    - System state validation (delegated to jobs)
72	    - Sequential job execution
73	    - Background job management (DiskSpaceMonitor)
74	    - Sync summary and session tracking
75	    """
76	
77	    def __init__(
78	        self,
79	        target: str,
80	        config: Configuration,
81	        *,
82	        auto_accept: bool = False,
83	        allow_consecutive: bool = False,
84	        dry_run: bool = False,
85	        allow_divergence: bool = False,
86	    ) -> None:
87	        """Initialize orchestrator with target and validated configuration.
88	
89	        Args:
90	            target: Target hostname or SSH alias
91	            config: Validated configuration from YAML file
92	            auto_accept: If True, auto-accept prompts (e.g., config sync)
93	            allow_consecutive: If True, skip warning about consecutive syncs
94	            dry_run: If True, preview sync without making changes
95	            allow_divergence: If True, skip the target-divergence guard (D-06 override)
96	        """
97	        self._config = config
98	        self._auto_accept = auto_accept
99	        self._allow_consecutive = allow_consecutive
100	        self._dry_run = dry_run
101	        self._allow_divergence = allow_divergence
102	        self._session_id = secrets.token_hex(4)
103	        self._session_folder = session_folder_name(self._session_id)
104	        self._source_hostname = get_local_hostname()
105	        self._target_hostname = target
106	
107	        # Core components
108	        self._event_bus = EventBus()
109	        self._logger = logging.getLogger("pcswitcher.orchestrator")
110	        self._connection: Connection | None = None
111	        self._local_executor: LocalExecutor | None = None
112	        self._remote_executor: RemoteExecutor | None = None
113	
114	        # Locks
115	        self._source_lock: SyncLock | None = None
116	        self._target_lock_process: RemoteProcess | None = None
117	
118	        # Background tasks
119	        self._task_group: asyncio.TaskGroup | None = None
120	        self._cleanup_in_progress = False
121	
122	        # Logging infrastructure (initialized in run())
123	        self._queue_listener: QueueListener | None = None
124	        self._ui: TerminalUI | None = None
125	        self._console: Console | None = None
126	        self._ui_task: asyncio.Task[None] | None = None

128	    def _create_job_context(self, config: dict[str, Any]) -> JobContext:
129	        """Create JobContext with current orchestrator state.
130	
131	        Must only be called after SSH connection is established (Phase 2+).
132	        """
133	        assert self._local_executor is not None
134	        assert self._remote_executor is not None
135	
136	        return JobContext(
137	            config=config,
138	            source=self._local_executor,
139	            target=self._remote_executor,
140	            event_bus=self._event_bus,
141	            session_id=self._session_id,
142	            source_hostname=self._source_hostname,
143	            target_hostname=self._target_hostname,
144	            dry_run=self._dry_run,
145	            allow_divergence=self._allow_divergence,
146	        )
147	
148	    async def run(self) -> SyncSession:  # noqa: PLR0915
149	        """Execute the complete sync workflow.
150	
151	        Returns:
152	            SyncSession with results and status
153	
154	        Raises:
155	            Various exceptions for critical failures (connection, locks, validation, etc.)
156	        """
157	        session = SyncSession(
158	            session_id=self._session_id,
159	            started_at=datetime.now(UTC),
160	            source_hostname=self._source_hostname,
161	            target_hostname=self._target_hostname,
162	            config={},  # TODO: Add config snapshot
163	            status=SessionStatus.RUNNING,
164	            job_results=[],
165	        )
166	
167	        # Initialize logging infrastructure BEFORE any operations
168	        # Both hostnames are known: source from local hostname, target from CLI argument
169	        if not self._source_hostname:
170	            raise RuntimeError("Source hostname is not set")
171	        if not self._target_hostname:
172	            raise RuntimeError("Target hostname is not set")
173	
174	        # Create log file path and set up stdlib logging infrastructure
175	        log_file_path = get_logs_directory() / generate_log_filename(self._session_id)
176	        self._queue_listener, _ = setup_logging(log_file_path, self._config.logging)
177	
178	        # Log session start with hostname mapping (LOG-FR-SESSION-HOSTNAMES)
179	        self._logger.info(
180	            "Starting sync session",
181	            extra={
182	                "job": "orchestrator",
183	                "host": "source",
184	                "source_hostname": self._source_hostname,
185	                "target_hostname": self._target_hostname,
186	                "session_id": self._session_id,
187	            },
188	        )
189	
190	        # Subscribe to event bus for UI (ProgressEvent, ConnectionEvent only)
191	        ui_queue = self._event_bus.subscribe()
192	
193	        # Calculate total steps: 8 system phases + sync jobs + 1 post-snapshot
194	        # System phases: 1=source lock, 2=SSH, 3=target lock, 4=validation,
195	        # 5=disk check, 6=pre-snapshots, 7=install on target, 8=config sync
196	        total_steps = 8 + len(self._config.sync_jobs) + 1
197	        self._console = Console()
198	        self._ui = TerminalUI(
199	            console=self._console,
200	            total_steps=total_steps,
201	        )
202	
203	        # Start UI event consumer as background task (ProgressEvent, ConnectionEvent)
204	        self._ui_task = asyncio.create_task(self._ui.consume_events(queue=ui_queue))
205	
206	        # Start UI live display
207	        self._ui.start()
208	
209	        # Log dry-run mode banner
210	        if self._dry_run:
211	            self._logger.info(
212	                "[DRY-RUN] Preview mode - no changes will be made",
213	                extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"},
214	            )
215	
216	        try:
217	            # Pre-Phase: Check for consecutive sync (before any operations)
218	            if not self._allow_consecutive:
219	                should_continue = await self._check_consecutive_sync()
220	                if not should_continue:
221	                    raise RuntimeError("Sync aborted: consecutive sync without receiving a sync back first")
222	
223	            # Phase 1: Acquire source lock
224	            self._logger.info("Acquiring source lock", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
225	            await self._acquire_source_lock()
226	            self._ui.set_current_step(1)
227	
228	            # Phase 2: Establish SSH connection
229	            self._logger.info("Connecting to target", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
230	            await self._establish_connection()
231	            assert self._remote_executor is not None
232	            self._ui.set_current_step(2)
233	
234	            # Phase 3: Acquire target lock
235	            self._logger.info("Acquiring target lock", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "target"})
236	            await self._acquire_target_lock()
237	            self._ui.set_current_step(3)
238	
239	            # Phase 4: Job discovery and validation
240	            self._logger.info("Discovering and validating jobs", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
241	            jobs = await self._discover_and_validate_jobs()
242	            self._ui.set_current_step(4)
243	
244	            # Phase 5: Disk space preflight check
245	            await self._check_disk_space_preflight()
246	            self._ui.set_current_step(5)
247	
248	            # Phase 6: Pre-sync snapshots
249	            self._logger.info("Creating pre-sync snapshots", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
250	            await self._create_snapshots(SnapshotPhase.PRE)
251	            self._ui.set_current_step(6)
252	
253	            # Phase 7: Install/upgrade pc-switcher on target (after snapshots for rollback safety)
254	            self._logger.info(
255	                "Ensuring pc-switcher is installed on target",
256	                extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "target"},
257	            )
258	            await self._install_on_target_job()
259	            self._ui.set_current_step(7)
260	
261	            # Phase 8: Sync config from source to target
262	            self._logger.info("Syncing configuration to target", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "target"})
263	            await self._sync_config_to_target()
264	            self._ui.set_current_step(8)
265	
266	            # Phase 9: Execute sync jobs with background monitoring
267	            self._logger.info("Starting sync operations", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
268	            job_results = await self._execute_jobs(jobs)
269	            session.job_results = job_results
270	
271	            # Phase 10: Post-sync snapshots
272	            self._logger.info("Creating post-sync snapshots", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
273	            await self._create_snapshots(SnapshotPhase.POST)
274	            self._ui.set_current_step(8 + len(jobs) + 1)
275	
276	            # Success - update sync history on both machines
277	            session.status = SessionStatus.COMPLETED
278	            session.ended_at = datetime.now(UTC)
279	            self._logger.info("Sync completed successfully", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
280	
281	            # Update sync history: this machine was SOURCE, target was TARGET.
282	            # Skipped in dry-run mode (D-12: dry-run must not write any state).
283	            if not self._dry_run:
284	                await self._update_sync_history()
285	
286	            return session
287	
288	        except asyncio.CancelledError:
289	            session.status = SessionStatus.INTERRUPTED
290	            session.ended_at = datetime.now(UTC)
291	            session.error_message = "Sync interrupted by user (SIGINT)"
292	            self._logger.warning("Sync interrupted by user", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
293	            raise
294	
295	        except Exception as e:
296	            session.status = SessionStatus.FAILED
297	            session.ended_at = datetime.now(UTC)
298	            session.error_message = str(e)
299	            self._logger.critical("Sync failed: %s", e, extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
300	            raise
301	
302	        finally:
303	            # Cleanup
304	            await self._cleanup()

306	    async def _acquire_source_lock(self) -> None:
307	        """Acquire exclusive lock on source machine.
308	
309	        Uses unified lock file that prevents this machine from participating
310	        in any other sync (as source or target) while this sync is running.
311	        """
312	        self._source_lock = SyncLock(get_lock_path())
313	
314	        holder_info = f"source:{self._source_hostname}:{self._session_id}"
315	        if not self._source_lock.acquire(holder_info):
316	            existing_holder = self._source_lock.get_holder_info()
317	            raise RuntimeError(f"This machine is already involved in a sync (held by: {existing_holder})")
318	
319	    async def _establish_connection(self) -> None:
320	        """Establish SSH connection to target machine."""
321	        self._connection = Connection(self._target_hostname, event_bus=self._event_bus)
322	        await self._connection.connect()
323	
324	        # Create executors
325	        self._local_executor = LocalExecutor()
326	        self._remote_executor = RemoteExecutor(self._connection.ssh_connection)
327	
328	        self._logger.info("Connected to target", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "target"})
329	
330	    async def _acquire_target_lock(self) -> None:
331	        """Acquire exclusive lock on target machine via SSH.
332	
333	        Uses the same unified lock file as the source, ensuring the target
334	        machine cannot participate in any other sync while this one runs.
335	        """
336	        assert self._remote_executor is not None
337	
338	        self._target_lock_process = await start_persistent_remote_lock(
339	            self._remote_executor, self._source_hostname, self._session_id
340	        )
341	        if self._target_lock_process is None:
342	            raise RuntimeError(f"Target {self._target_hostname} is already involved in a sync")

391	    async def _check_consecutive_sync(self) -> bool:
392	        """Check if this is a consecutive sync and prompt user if so.
393	
394	        A consecutive sync is when this machine tries to be a SOURCE again
395	        without having been a TARGET first. This usually means the user forgot
396	        to sync back from the other machine.
397	
398	        Returns:
399	            True if sync should continue, False if user aborted.
400	        """
401	        assert self._console is not None
402	        assert self._ui is not None
403	
404	        last_role, had_error = get_last_role_with_error()
405	
406	        # No warning needed if:
407	        # - No history exists (first sync)
408	        # - Last role was TARGET (received a sync, now sending - normal flow)
409	        if last_role is None and not had_error:
410	            return True
411	        if last_role == SyncRole.TARGET:
412	            return True
413	
414	        # Warning needed if:
415	        # - Last role was SOURCE (consecutive sync from same machine)
416	        # - History file was corrupted (safety-first: treat as consecutive)
417	
418	        # In non-interactive mode (no TTY), use the default "n" response
419	        # to avoid hanging on Prompt.ask()
420	        if not sys.stdin.isatty():
421	            self._console.print(
422	                "[yellow]Warning: Consecutive sync detected (no back-sync received).[/yellow]\n"
423	                "Use --allow-consecutive to override in non-interactive mode."
424	            )
425	            return False
426	
427	        self._ui.stop()
428	
429	        try:
430	            self._console.print()
431	            self._console.print(
432	                Panel(
433	                    "[yellow]Warning: You are syncing FROM this machine again "
434	                    "without receiving a sync back first.[/yellow]\n\n"
435	                    "The normal workflow is:\n"
436	                    "  1. Sync FROM this machine TO another\n"
437	                    "  2. Work on the other machine\n"
438	                    "  3. Sync FROM the other machine BACK to this one\n"
439	                    "  4. Then sync FROM this machine again\n\n"
440	                    "You appear to be at step 4 without completing step 3.\n"
441	                    "Continuing may overwrite changes made on the target machine.",
442	                    title="Consecutive Sync Warning",
443	                    border_style="yellow",
444	                )
445	            )
446	            self._console.print()
447	
448	            response = Prompt.ask(
449	                "[bold]Continue anyway?[/bold]",
450	                choices=["y", "n"],
451	                default="n",
452	            )
453	
454	            return response.lower() == "y"
455	        finally:
456	            self._ui.start()
457	
458	    async def _update_sync_history(self) -> None:
459	        """Update sync history on both source and target machines.
460	
461	        After a successful sync:
462	        - Source machine's history: last_role = SOURCE
463	        - Target machine's history: last_role = TARGET
464	
465	        This enables the consecutive sync warning to work correctly.
466	
467	        Raises:
468	            RuntimeError: If history update fails on either machine.
469	        """
470	        # Update local (source) history
471	        record_role(SyncRole.SOURCE)
472	        self._logger.debug("Updated sync history: role=source", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
473	
474	        # Update remote (target) history via SSH
475	        if self._remote_executor is not None:
476	            cmd = get_record_role_command(SyncRole.TARGET)
477	            result = await self._remote_executor.run_command(cmd)
478	            if not result.success:
479	                raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to update sync history on target: {result.stderr}")
480	            self._logger.debug("Updated sync history: role=target", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "target"})
481	
482	    async def _discover_and_validate_jobs(self) -> list[Job]:
483	        """Discover enabled jobs from config and validate their configuration.
484	
485	        Dynamically imports job modules based on enabled jobs in config.
486	        Convention: job_name == module_name (e.g., "dummy_success" → pcswitcher.jobs.dummy_success)
487	
488	        Returns:
489	            List of job instances ready for execution
490	
491	        Raises:
492	            RuntimeError: If any job config validation fails
493	        """
494	        jobs: list[Job] = []
495	        config_errors: list[ConfigError] = []
496	
497	        # Log entire config at DEBUG level
498	        self._logger.debug(
499	            "Configuration loaded",
500	            extra={
501	                "job": "orchestrator",
502	                "host": "source",
503	                "logging_file": self._config.logging.file,
504	                "logging_tui": self._config.logging.tui,
505	                "logging_external": self._config.logging.external,
506	                "sync_jobs": self._config.sync_jobs,
507	                "disk_preflight_minimum": self._config.disk.preflight_minimum,
508	                "disk_runtime_minimum": self._config.disk.runtime_minimum,
509	                "disk_warning_threshold": self._config.disk.warning_threshold,
510	                "disk_check_interval": self._config.disk.check_interval,
511	                "btrfs_subvolumes": self._config.btrfs_snapshots.subvolumes,
512	                "btrfs_keep_recent": self._config.btrfs_snapshots.keep_recent,
513	                "btrfs_max_age_days": self._config.btrfs_snapshots.max_age_days,
514	            },
515	        )
516	
517	        # Lazy load only enabled jobs (job_name == module_name)
518	        for job_name, enabled in self._config.sync_jobs.items():
519	            if not enabled:
520	                self._logger.debug(
521	                    "Job %s is disabled in config",
522	                    job_name,
523	                    extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"},
524	                )
525	                continue
526	
527	            # Dynamic import: pcswitcher.jobs.{job_name}
528	            try:
529	                module = importlib.import_module(f"pcswitcher.jobs.{job_name}")
530	            except ModuleNotFoundError:
531	                self._logger.warning(
532	                    "Job module pcswitcher.jobs.%s not found",
533	                    job_name,
534	                    extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"},
535	                )
536	                continue
537	
538	            # Find the SyncJob class in the module with matching name
539	            job_class: type[SyncJob] | None = None
540	            for attr_name in dir(module):
541	                attr = getattr(module, attr_name)
542	                if (
543	                    isinstance(attr, type)
544	                    and issubclass(attr, SyncJob)
545	                    and attr is not SyncJob
546	                    and getattr(attr, "name", None) == job_name
547	                ):
548	                    job_class = attr
549	                    break
550	
551	            if job_class is None:
552	                self._logger.warning(
553	                    "No SyncJob with name=%s found in module pcswitcher.jobs.%s",
554	                    job_name,
555	                    job_name,
556	                    extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"},
557	                )
558	                continue
559	
560	            # Validate job config (Phase 2)
561	            job_config = self._config.job_configs.get(job_name, {})
562	            errors = job_class.validate_config(job_config)
563	            if errors:
564	                config_errors.extend(errors)
565	            else:
566	                context = self._create_job_context(job_config)
567	                jobs.append(job_class(context))
568	
569	        # Check for config errors
570	        if config_errors:
571	            error_msgs = [f"  - {e.job}: {e.path} - {e.message}" for e in config_errors]
572	            raise RuntimeError("Job configuration validation failed:\n" + "\n".join(error_msgs))
573	
574	        # Validate system state for all jobs (Phase 3)
575	        validation_errors: list[ValidationError] = []
576	        for job in jobs:
577	            errors = await job.validate()
578	            if errors:
579	                validation_errors.extend(errors)
580	
581	        if validation_errors:
582	            error_msgs = [f"  - {e.job} ({e.host.value}): {e.message}" for e in validation_errors]
583	            raise RuntimeError("System state validation failed:\n" + "\n".join(error_msgs))
584	
585	        return jobs

587	    async def _check_disk_space_preflight(self) -> None:
588	        """Check disk space on both source and target before creating snapshots.
589	
590	        Per CORE-FR-DISK-PRE, verifies both hosts have sufficient free disk space
591	        based on the configured preflight_minimum threshold.
592	
593	        Raises:
594	            RuntimeError: If either host has insufficient disk space
595	        """
596	        assert self._local_executor is not None
597	        assert self._remote_executor is not None
598	
599	        self._logger.info("Checking disk space on both hosts", extra={"job": "orchestrator", "host": "source"})
600	
601	        # Parse threshold once (same for both hosts)
602	        threshold_type, threshold_value = parse_threshold(self._config.disk.preflight_minimum)
603	
604	        # Check both hosts in parallel
605	        source_task = check_disk_space(self._local_executor, "/")
606	        target_task = check_disk_space(self._remote_executor, "/")
607	        source_disk, target_disk = await asyncio.gather(source_task, target_task)
608	
609	        # Helper to format bytes in human-readable form
610	        def format_bytes(bytes_value: int) -> str:
611	            value = float(bytes_value)
612	            for unit in ["B", "KiB", "MiB", "GiB", "TiB"]:
613	                if value < 1024:
614	                    return f"{value:.1f}{unit}"
615	                value /= 1024
616	            return f"{value:.1f}PiB"
617	
618	        # Helper to check if disk space is sufficient
619	        def is_sufficient(disk_space: DiskSpace, threshold_type: str, threshold_value: int) -> bool:
620	            if threshold_type == "percent":
621	                # Threshold is percentage of total disk that must be free
622	                free_percent = (disk_space.available_bytes / disk_space.total_bytes) * 100
623	                return free_percent >= threshold_value
624	            else:  # bytes
625	                return disk_space.available_bytes >= threshold_value
626	
627	        # Helper to format free space description
628	        def format_free_space(disk_space: DiskSpace) -> str:
629	            free_bytes = format_bytes(disk_space.available_bytes)
630	            free_percent = (disk_space.available_bytes / disk_space.total_bytes) * 100
